fields.description:
## Why
The design has sharp edges (hot-path cost, concurrency on the shared branch, rate-limit,
no_mutate, absent marker). Only tests lock the invariants so future ensures can be added
safely.

## What
Add behavioral tests covering: git-level idempotency (`run_ensures` twice on a converged store
→ zero new commits); hot-path regression (a common mutation never invokes `run_ensures`, spawns
no git-config subprocess, reads `.ensure-applied` at most once per process); concurrency (two processes running the
sweep on the same checkout serialize under the write lock and converge; `.ensure-applied`
written atomically, no torn/lost state); no_mutate (library read-only fsck mutates nothing);
absent/garbage `.ensure-applied` (→ empty set → converges, no re-commit storm). The fsck byte-parity/help-golden tests are owned by WS3, and the pending-hint unit tests by WS2 — NOT here.

## Scope
In: the behavioral test suite for WS1-2 invariants.
Out: fsck byte-parity/help goldens (WS3), the feature code itself (WS1-3), docs (WS4).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Idempotency test: `run_ensures()` twice on a converged store makes zero new commits/pushes (git-log assertion)
- [ ] Hot-path regression test: a ticket mutation never calls `run_ensures`, spawns no git-config subprocess, and reads `.ensure-applied` at most once per process
- [ ] Concurrency test: two real OS processes sweeping the same checkout serialize under the store write lock (`_store/lock.py`) and converge; `.ensure-applied` is written atomically (no torn/lost writes)
- [ ] no_mutate test: a library read-only fsck surface (no_mutate=True) mutates nothing and never runs the sweep
- [ ] absent/garbage-marker test: a missing or garbage `.ensure-applied` degrades to empty-set (pending=all) without crashing
